content_en:
“What have you responded to the Messengers?”

for He will certainly ask us about it and demand us to answer lim; Allah, Most High, said,

“Truly, We will ask those whom we sent them the Messengers and We shall also ask the Ones We sent to them”

The Prophet (38) said: “It was revealed to me that you will be tested and asked about me” , meaning that we will be asked about

" Ahmad and others It was declared hasan by al-Albani

HEARTFELT ADVICE TO A FRIEND

him in our graves.

Hence whoever is aware of any act of Sunnah then abandoned it for the sake of someone else’s view, he will be questioned about it and then will know the consequences of his actions.

Fourth Element: Station of Excellence (a/ihsan) and Vigilance of Allah (a/muraqaba)

Station of excellence (a/-shsan)' renders to mean the state of being vigilant of Allah (a/-muraqaba)* - th: t is to say, to worship Him as if you could see Him [watching ) |}. This state stems from having a perfect belief (Aamal al-iman) in Allah, His Names and Attributes; to the point that you become as if you can envision Him above His Throne above the heavens; arranging the affairs

of the entire creation and issuing His commands that governs _-”

the cosmos, all the while, the deeds and souls of His slaves are presented before Him. In this state, a believing servant witnesses all that with his heart, and experiences [the application of] His Attributes and Names; he sees [in his heart and mind] that He is the Sustainer [of all that exists] (a/-Qayyam), the Ever-Living

, excellence. To worship Allah as if one is seeing Him, and

Owing that even though one sees Him not, He sees the servant.

* Muragabah: self-inspection. The servant having the sure knowledge that Allah Sees him in all cir

cumstances and knows all that he is doing, as such the hq does his utmost not to fall into the prohibited matters and to correct his own failings.

HEARTFELT ADVICE TOA FRIEND

(al-Hayy), the All-Hearing (a/-Sami'), the All-Seeing (a/Basir), the All-Powerful and Strong (a/-‘Agz), the All-Wise (a/-Hakim), and the Commander Who has authority over everything; Who Loves, Hates and Be Pleased; Who angers and does all that He is pleased to do; Who decrees all that He wants while being above His Throne; and nothing or anyone escapes from His Knowledge for He knows that which deceives the eyes and what the breasts conceal.

The state of mindfulness is the foundation from which all the deeds of the heart emanate as it necessitates becoming humble (al-haya’) and shy (a/-ijlal from Allah, respectful (a-/agim) to Him, and turning (a/-inabah)* to Him. Furthermore, it calls forth one’s love (al-mahabba) of Allah, fear (a/-khashiya) of Him, [full] reliance (al-tawakkul) on Him, submission (a/-kbusha')> to Him and the feeling of humility (a/-du/). It also/ends all the whispers (a/-waswasa)

> Haya’: modesty, derived from the word Aayd?, or life because it is through modesty that the heart is granted life and it is through the absence of modesty that it dies. It is a state that arises through the servant being aware that Allah is watching him, having love, fear and awe of Him and thinking little of himself. Al-Junayd said, ‘a/-Hayda’ is to recognise the bounties of Allah and then to recognise ones own shortcomings. Through this a state is engendered which is termed a/-Haya’, the reality of which is that it is a mannerism that prevents one from committing vile actions and from being lax in fulfilling the rights of Allah.’

* Inaba: returning, ibn al-Qayyim, Madani as-Salikin [1/467] said, ‘indbah comprises four matters: the love of Allah, submission to Him, turning to Him, and turning away from everything besides Him. A person cannot be said to “penitent” unless he meets all four requirements and the explanation of the Salaf to this word revolves around this. The word also carries the meaning of quickness, returning and precedence; therefore the penitent is rushing to do that which would please his Lord, turning back to Him at every moment and foremost in doing that which He loves.’

Fourth Element: Station of Excellence (al-ihsan) and Vigilance of Allah

of devils, casts away the doubts and commits the heart and the mind to become devoted to Allah alone.

How close a servant is from his Lord depends on his share from the state of thsan, and because of which, the reward and rank of salah differ from one to another so that the difference between the salah of one person and another can be like the distance between heavens and earth though both perform the sa/ah similarly.

Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of Allah

It is to acknowledge the infinite blessing for being at the service of Allah. That your heart and body are deployed to serve Him [alone] - and are only for Allah, whose Grace and Favour were conferred upc’ vou, to facilitate for you being in such a state [of servitude]. Had it not been for Allah’s Grace and Favour upon you, nothing of that would have occurred, which is something that the companions of the Prophet (#8) acknowledged when they used to repeat a line of poetry, that reads as follows: .

‘By Allah, was it not for Allah, neither would we have been guided, nor able to give charity nor perform the prayer’

Allah, Most High, said:

i Kesuaeitess of (Zithacieg 2s

“They consider it a favour to you that they have ac-

Fifth Element: Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of Allah

cepted Islam. Say, ‘Do not consider your Islam a favour to me. Rather, Allah has conferred favour upon you

that He has guided you to the faith, if you should be truthful.”

It is Allah who permitted a servant, to become a believer in the first instance. It is Him who facilitated for people to pray, just as Prophet Ibrahim ——s said in the ayah,

OF bas cA oy Ee Sere roe eel onanoy

“Our Lord!, And make us Muslims [in submission] to

You and from our descendants a Muslim nation fin submission] to You”

“My Lord, make me one who establislicrs the prayer, and [many] from my descendants.”

It is therefore a fact that it’s the favour (a/-minna) of Allah that He conferred upon His slaves to worship and obey Him. Indeed,

this is from the greatest of Allah’s graces upon His servant: Allah, Most High, said,

“And whatever you have of favour - it is from Allah”

HEARTFELT ADVICE TO A FRIEND

ese 7 gs meee

paul teisgiie Sc all eal

“And [remember] when your Lord proclaimed, ‘If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favour]; but if

you deny, indeed, My punishment is severe.” [Fussilat (41): 7]

This element by far is one of the greatest elements and most beneficial for the servant of Allah; so the more profound the Tawhid of a person is, the more perfected his share from this element will be. One of the benefits attained from this element is that it prevents the heaft from feeling egoistic about the good deeds performed because when a person acknowledges and affirms that Allah, Most High, is the One who guided the servant to Him and that it is His favour upon the servant that he is a believer, he will consequently no: ' = fascinated by his good deeds or even feel superior to other peopie because of that; therefore all such debased feelings are instantly removed from his heart and so neither does he talk about them nor feel better than others because of them; and this is the nature of any accepted good deed.

From the other benefits of acknowledging Allah’s favour upon us, is that it makes a person praise the One really entitled and deserving of praise; not praise one self but rather acknowledges the fact that all the praise is due to Allah alone just as one acknowledges the fact that the grace he basks in and the bounties one is blessed with are from Allah alone. This benefit manifests the perfection (tamam) of one’s tawhid of Allah.

That being said, a person will never have a firm foothold in the rank (maqam) of tawhid, until after one acknowledges and experi-

Fifth Element: Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of Allah

ences all what has been said. As soon as one comes to cognize this, one deeply roots his footing on its ground, and as soon as the heart experiences that, one will then reap its fruit that no worldly grace or blessing can ever match, i.e. a person will then lavish in the (a/-mababba) love and intimacy (a/-uns) of Allah while basking in the amiability of His company and longing (a/-shawq) to the moment of meeting Him, and luxuriate in His remembrance and worship. Truly, there is no good in life for a person when the path leading to all that is blocked and when the heart is shunned from all that as a person of this kind is as Allah described in the ayah,

“Let them eat and enjoy sche and be diverted by [false] hope, for they are going to know.”

Sixth Element: Blaming One’s Self for Inadequate Efforts

It is to maintain the feeling that your endeavours and efforts to worship Allah are always inadequate; and that the right of Allah upon you is greater; therefore your obedience, worship and servitude (a/-‘wbiidiya) must be greater than what you perform, as the level of you: _vitude to Allah must befit His Glory and Power.

If the servants and subjects of kings exhibit high regards (a/- ta’zim), respect (al-ibtiram), reverence (a/-tangir), awe (al-khashiya), dread (a/-mahaba) and sincerity (a/-nush) towards their masters to the extent they dedicate their body-limbs and Learts to serve them, then it befits more that the King of all kings (Malik al-Maluk) and the Lord of the heavens and the earth be treated not only in a similar manner but far more superior.

If a servant (a/-‘abd) acknowledges that his state of servitude (a/- “budiya) does not fulfil, or is even close to fulfilling the due rights of Allah upon him. A person‘will then realise their shortcomings and will thus hasten to seek His forgiveness, apologise for ones deficiencies, inadequate worship and obedience. It is for this reason we are in more need of His forgiveness due to our inadequate worship and obedience than we are in need of asking His reward

Sixth Element: Blaming One’s Self for Inadequate E; sfforts

for the worship and obedience we have performed. There is no pride or credit to be claimed by a person, even if one was able to fulfil all the requirements of his state of servitude (al-‘ubidiya) to Allah, Most High, because in that case one would only be fulfill ling the natural (role) of his status as a servant to his Master, Allah Glorified be He, requires. It is obvious that if a servant served his master then he requested from his master a reward for his service, people would have deemed him a.fool though in the true terms and sense of reality he is nota truly a slave of another man since mankind is owned and is wholly a servant of Allah alone from all aspects. That being said, when Allah rewatds His servants for their worship, which they are obligated to perform anyhow - as His servants - then that is due to Allah’s favour and grace that He
content_id:
"Apa yang kalian jawab kepada para Rasul?"

karena Dia pasti akan menanyakan kita tentang hal itu dan menuntut kita untuk menjawab; Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

"Sungguh, Kami akan menanyai orang-orang yang telah Kami utus kepada mereka para Rasul dan Kami juga akan menanyai para Rasul yang Kami utus"

Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda: "Telah diwahyukan kepadaku bahwa kalian akan diuji dan ditanya tentang diriku", artinya kita akan ditanya tentang

" Ahmad dan lainnya. Dinyatakan hasan oleh al-Albani

NASIHAT TULUS KEPADA SAHABAT

beliau di dalam kubur kita.

Maka barangsiapa yang mengetahui suatu amalan Sunnah lalu meninggalkannya demi mengikuti pendapat orang lain, ia akan ditanya tentang hal itu dan akan mengetahui akibat dari perbuatannya.

Unsur Keempat: Kedudukan Ihsan (al-ihsan) dan Pengawasan Allah (al-muraqabah)

Kedudukan ihsan (al-ihsan) bermakna keadaan senantiasa mengawasi diri terhadap Allah (al-muraqabah) — yaitu beribadah kepada-Nya seolah-olah engkau melihat-Nya. Keadaan ini berasal dari kesempurnaan iman (kamal al-iman) kepada Allah, Nama-nama dan Sifat-sifat-Nya; hingga pada titik di mana engkau seolah-olah dapat membayangkan-Nya di atas Arsy-Nya di atas langit; mengatur segala urusan

seluruh makhluk dan mengeluarkan perintah-perintah-Nya yang mengatur alam semesta, sementara itu, amal perbuatan dan jiwa para hamba-Nya diperlihatkan di hadapan-Nya. Dalam keadaan ini, seorang hamba yang beriman menyaksikan semua itu dengan hatinya, dan merasakan [penerapan] Sifat-sifat dan Nama-nama-Nya; ia melihat [dengan hati dan pikirannya] bahwa Dia adalah Pemelihara [segala yang ada] (al-Qayyum), Yang Maha Hidup

, ihsan. Beribadah kepada Allah seolah-olah seseorang melihat-Nya, dan

mengetahui bahwa meskipun seseorang tidak melihat-Nya, Dia melihat hamba tersebut.

* Muraqabah: introspeksi diri. Hamba yang memiliki keyakinan pasti bahwa Allah melihatnya dalam segala keadaan dan mengetahui segala yang ia lakukan, sehingga ia berusaha sekuat tenaga untuk tidak terjerumus ke dalam perkara-perkara yang diharamkan dan memperbaiki kekurangan-kekurangannya sendiri.

NASIHAT TULUS KEPADA SAHABAT

(al-Hayy), Yang Maha Mendengar (al-Sami'), Yang Maha Melihat (al-Bashir), Yang Maha Kuasa dan Maha Kuat (al-'Aziz), Yang Maha Bijaksana (al-Hakim), dan Pemerintah yang memiliki kekuasaan atas segala sesuatu; Yang Mencintai, Membenci dan Meridhai; Yang murka dan melakukan segala yang Dia ridhai; Yang menetapkan segala yang Dia kehendaki sementara berada di atas Arsy-Nya; dan tidak ada sesuatu pun atau siapa pun yang luput dari Pengetahuan-Nya karena Dia mengetahui pandangan mata yang khianat dan apa yang disembunyikan oleh dada.

Keadaan muraqabah adalah landasan dari mana seluruh amalan hati bersumber, sebab hal itu meniscayakan seseorang menjadi rendah hati (al-haya') dan malu (al-ijlal) kepada Allah, hormat (al-ta'zhim) kepada-Nya, dan kembali (al-inabah)* kepada-Nya. Lebih jauh lagi, hal itu melahirkan kecintaan (al-mahabbah) kepada Allah, rasa takut (al-khashyah) kepada-Nya, tawakkul (al-tawakkul) kepada-Nya, ketundukan (al-khusyu')> kepada-Nya dan perasaan kerendahan hati (al-dzull). Hal itu juga menghilangkan segala bisikan (al-waswasah)

> Haya': rasa malu, berasal dari kata hayah, atau kehidupan, karena melalui rasa malu itulah hati diberikan kehidupan dan melalui ketiadaan rasa malu itulah hati mati. Ia adalah keadaan yang muncul ketika seorang hamba menyadari bahwa Allah mengawasinya, memiliki cinta, takut dan rasa kagum kepada-Nya serta merendahkan diri sendiri. Al-Junayd berkata, 'al-Haya' adalah mengenali nikmat-nikmat Allah kemudian mengenali kekurangan-kekurangan diri sendiri. Melalui ini lahirlah suatu keadaan yang disebut al-Haya', yang hakikatnya adalah sebuah adab yang mencegah seseorang dari melakukan perbuatan keji dan dari lalai dalam memenuhi hak-hak Allah.'

* Inabah: kembali, Ibnu al-Qayyim, Madarij as-Salikin [1/467] berkata, 'inabah mencakup empat perkara: kecintaan kepada Allah, ketundukan kepada-Nya, kembali kepada-Nya, dan berpaling dari segala sesuatu selain-Nya. Seseorang tidak bisa dikatakan "bertaubat" kecuali ia memenuhi keempat persyaratan ini dan penjelasan para Salaf terhadap kata ini berkisar pada hal ini. Kata ini juga mengandung makna kecepatan, kembali dan mendahului; oleh karena itu orang yang bertaubat adalah yang bersegera melakukan apa yang meridhakan Tuhannya, kembali kepada-Nya di setiap saat dan terdepan dalam melakukan apa yang Dia cintai.'

Unsur Keempat: Kedudukan Ihsan (al-ihsan) dan Pengawasan Allah

setan, menepis keraguan dan mengikat hati serta pikiran untuk mengabdi kepada Allah semata.

Seberapa dekat seorang hamba dengan Tuhannya bergantung pada bagiannya dari keadaan ihsan, dan karena itulah pahala dan kedudukan salah berbeda antara satu orang dengan yang lain sehingga perbedaan antara salah seseorang dengan orang lain bisa seperti jarak antara langit dan bumi meskipun keduanya melakukan salah dengan cara yang serupa.

Pengakuan terhadap Karunia (al-minnah) Allah

Yaitu mengakui nikmat yang tak terhingga karena berada dalam pengabdian kepada Allah. Bahwa hati dan tubuhmu dikerahkan untuk melayani-Nya [semata] — dan hanya untuk Allah, yang Karunia dan Anugerah-Nya dicurahkan kepadamu, untuk memudahkanmu berada dalam keadaan [penghambaan] seperti itu. Seandainya bukan karena Karunia dan Anugerah Allah kepadamu, tidak satu pun dari itu akan terjadi, yang mana hal ini diakui oleh para sahabat Nabi (ﷺ) ketika mereka biasa mengulang bait syair yang berbunyi sebagai berikut:

'Demi Allah, seandainya bukan karena Allah, kami tidak akan mendapat petunjuk, tidak mampu bersedekah dan tidak mampu melaksanakan salah'

Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

"Mereka menganggapnya sebagai kebaikan kepadamu bahwa mereka telah me-

Unsur Kelima: Pengakuan terhadap Karunia (al-minnah) Allah

meluk Islam. Katakanlah, 'Janganlah kalian menganggap keislaman kalian sebagai kebaikan kepadaku. Justru Allah-lah yang memberikan karunia kepada kalian

dengan menunjuki kalian kepada iman, jika kalian orang-orang yang benar.'"

Allah-lah yang memberikan izin kepada seorang hamba untuk menjadi seorang mukmin sejak awal. Dia-lah yang memudahkan manusia untuk salah, sebagaimana Nabi Ibrahim عليه السلام bersabda dalam ayat,

"Ya Tuhan kami!, Dan jadikanlah kami berdua orang yang berserah diri [tunduk] kepada-

Mu dan dari keturunan kami umat yang berserah diri [tunduk] kepada-Mu"

"Ya Tuhanku, jadikanlah aku dan anak cucuku orang-orang yang tetap mendirikan salah."

Oleh karena itu, merupakan suatu kenyataan bahwa karunia (al-minnah) Allah-lah yang Dia curahkan kepada hamba-hamba-Nya untuk beribadah dan menaati-Nya. Sungguh,

ini termasuk karunia Allah yang paling agung kepada hamba-Nya: Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

"Dan apa saja nikmat yang ada pada kamu — maka itu adalah dari Allah"

NASIHAT TULUS KEPADA SAHABAT

"Dan [ingatlah] ketika Tuhanmu memaklumkan, 'Jika kamu bersyukur, niscaya Aku akan menambah [nikmat] kepadamu; tetapi jika

kamu mengingkari, sesungguhnya azab-Ku sangat berat.'" [Fushshilat (41): 7]

Unsur ini sejauh ini merupakan salah satu unsur yang paling agung dan paling bermanfaat bagi hamba Allah; maka semakin mendalam tawhid seseorang, semakin sempurna bagiannya dari unsur ini. Salah satu manfaat yang diperoleh dari unsur ini adalah ia mencegah hati dari merasa sombong atas amal kebaikan yang dilakukan, karena ketika seseorang mengakui dan membenarkan bahwa Allah Ta'ala-lah yang menunjuki hamba kepada-Nya dan bahwa itu adalah karunia-Nya kepada hamba bahwa ia menjadi seorang mukmin, ia tidak akan terpesona oleh amal kebaikannya atau bahkan merasa lebih tinggi dari orang lain karena hal itu; oleh karena itu segala perasaan rendah seperti itu seketika tersingkir dari hatinya sehingga ia tidak membicarakannya dan tidak merasa lebih baik dari orang lain karenanya; dan inilah sifat dari setiap amal kebaikan yang diterima.

Di antara manfaat lain dari mengakui karunia Allah kepada kita adalah hal itu membuat seseorang memuji Dzat yang benar-benar berhak dan layak dipuji; bukan memuji diri sendiri melainkan mengakui fakta bahwa segala pujian hanya milik Allah semata, sebagaimana seseorang mengakui fakta bahwa karunia yang ia nikmati dan nikmat yang ia terima adalah dari Allah semata. Manfaat ini menampakkan kesempurnaan (tamam) tawhid seseorang kepada Allah.

Dengan demikian, seseorang tidak akan pernah memiliki pijakan yang kokoh dalam kedudukan (maqam) tawhid, kecuali setelah ia mengakui dan merasakan

Unsur Kelima: Pengakuan terhadap Karunia (al-minnah) Allah

semua yang telah disebutkan. Begitu seseorang menyadari hal ini, ia mengukuhkan pijakannya di atas landasan itu, dan begitu hati merasakannya, ia akan menuai buahnya yang tidak ada satu pun nikmat atau karunia duniawi yang dapat menandinginya, yaitu seseorang akan dilimpahi kecintaan (al-mahabbah) dan keintiman (al-uns) dengan Allah sambil menikmati keramahan kebersamaan-Nya dan kerinduan (al-syawq) kepada saat perjumpaan dengan-Nya, serta bersenang-senang dalam mengingat dan beribadah kepada-Nya. Sungguh, tidak ada kebaikan dalam hidup bagi seseorang ketika jalan menuju semua itu terhalang dan ketika hati dijauhkan dari semua itu, karena orang seperti ini sebagaimana Allah gambarkan dalam ayat,

"Biarkanlah mereka makan dan bersenang-senang dan dilalaikan oleh angan-angan [palsu], kelak mereka akan mengetahui."

Unsur Keenam: Menyalahkan Diri Sendiri atas Upaya yang Kurang Memadai

Yaitu senantiasa memelihara perasaan bahwa usaha dan upayamu dalam beribadah kepada Allah selalu kurang memadai; dan bahwa hak Allah atasmu lebih besar; oleh karena itu ketaatan, ibadah dan penghambaan (al-'ubudiyyah) harus lebih besar dari apa yang kamu lakukan, sebab tingkat penghambaan kepada Allah harus sesuai dengan Keagungan dan Kekuasaan-Nya.

Jika para pelayan dan rakyat dari raja-raja menunjukkan penghormatan yang tinggi (al-ta'zhim), penghargaan (al-ihtiram), pengagungan (al-tauqir), rasa kagum (al-khashyah), rasa gentar (al-mahabah) dan ketulusan (al-nush) terhadap tuan-tuan mereka hingga mereka mendedikasikan anggota tubuh dan hati mereka untuk melayani mereka, maka lebih layak lagi Raja segala raja (Malik al-Muluk) dan Tuhan langit dan bumi diperlakukan bukan hanya dengan cara yang serupa tetapi jauh lebih agung.

Jika seorang hamba (al-'abd) mengakui bahwa keadaan penghambaan (al-'ubudiyyah) yang ia jalani tidak memenuhi, atau bahkan tidak mendekati pemenuhan hak-hak Allah yang semestinya atasnya, ia akan menyadari kekurangan-kekurangannya dan dengan demikian bersegera memohon ampunan-Nya, meminta maaf atas segala kekurangan, ibadah dan ketaatan yang kurang memadai. Karena alasan inilah kita lebih membutuhkan ampunan-Nya akibat ibadah dan ketaatan kita yang kurang memadai daripada kita membutuhkan pahala-Nya

Unsur Keenam: Menyalahkan Diri Sendiri atas Upaya yang Kurang Memadai

atas ibadah dan ketaatan yang telah kita lakukan. Tidak ada kebanggaan atau penghargaan yang layak diklaim oleh seseorang, meskipun ia mampu memenuhi semua tuntutan keadaan penghambaan (al-'ubudiyyah) kepada Allah Ta'ala, karena dalam hal itu ia hanya memenuhi peran alami dari kedudukannya sebagai hamba kepada Tuannya, yang Allah — Maha Suci Dia — tetapkan. Sudah jelas bahwa jika seorang pelayan melayani tuannya lalu ia meminta imbalan atas pelayanannya, orang-orang akan menganggapnya bodoh meskipun dalam pengertian yang sebenarnya ia bukanlah benar-benar budak dari manusia lain karena manusia adalah milik dan sepenuhnya hamba Allah semata dari segala aspek. Dengan demikian, ketika Allah memberi pahala kepada hamba-hamba-Nya atas ibadah mereka, yang memang wajib mereka lakukan — sebagai hamba-hamba-Nya — maka itu adalah karena karunia dan anugerah Allah yang Dia
content_ar:
“What have you responded to the Messengers?”

for He will certainly ask us about it and demand us to answer lim; الله, Most High, said,

“Truly, We will ask those whom we sent them the Messengers and We shall also ask the Ones We sent to them”

النبي ﷺ (38) said: “It was revealed to me that you will be tested and asked about me” , meaning that we will be asked about

" Ahmad and others It was declared hasan by al-Albani

HEARTFELT ADVICE TO A FRIEND

him in our graves.

Hence whoever is aware of any act of السنة النبوية then abandoned it for the sake of someone else’s view, he will be questioned about it and then will know the consequences of his actions.

Fourth Element: Station of Excellence (a/ihsan) and Vigilance of الله (a/muraqaba)

Station of excellence (a/-shsan)' renders to mean the state of being vigilant of الله (a/-muraqaba)* - th: t is to say, to worship Him as if you could see Him [watching ) |}. This state stems from having a perfect belief (Aamal al-iman) in الله, His Names and Attributes; to the point that you become as if you can envision Him above His Throne above the heavens; arranging the affairs

of the entire creation and issuing His commands that governs _-”

the cosmos, all the while, the deeds and souls of His slaves are presented before Him. In this state, a believing servant witnesses all that with his heart, and experiences [the application of] His Attributes and Names; he sees [in his heart and mind] that He is the Sustainer [of all that exists] (a/-Qayyam), the Ever-Living

, excellence. To worship الله as if one is seeing Him, and

Owing that even though one sees Him not, He sees the servant.

* Muragabah: self-inspection. The servant having the sure knowledge that الله Sees him in all cir

cumstances and knows all that he is doing, as such the hq does his utmost not to fall into the prohibited matters and to correct his own failings.

HEARTFELT ADVICE TOA FRIEND

(al-Hayy), the All-Hearing (a/-Sami'), the All-Seeing (a/Basir), the All-Powerful and Strong (a/-‘Agz), the All-Wise (a/-Hakim), and the Commander Who has authority over everything; Who Loves, Hates and Be Pleased; Who angers and does all that He is pleased to do; Who decrees all that He wants while being above His Throne; and nothing or anyone escapes from His Knowledge for He knows that which deceives the eyes and what the breasts conceal.

The state of mindfulness is the foundation from which all the deeds of the heart emanate as it necessitates becoming humble (al-haya’) and shy (a/-ijlal from الله, respectful (a-/agim) to Him, and turning (a/-inabah)* to Him. Furthermore, it calls forth one’s love (al-mahabba) of الله, fear (a/-khashiya) of Him, [full] reliance (al-tawakkul) on Him, submission (a/-kbusha')> to Him and the feeling of humility (a/-du/). It also/ends all the whispers (a/-waswasa)

> Haya’: modesty, derived from the word Aayd?, or life because it is through modesty that the heart is granted life and it is through the absence of modesty that it dies. It is a state that arises through the servant being aware that الله is watching him, having love, fear and awe of Him and thinking little of himself. Al-Junayd said, ‘a/-Hayda’ is to recognise the bounties of الله and then to recognise ones own shortcomings. Through this a state is engendered which is termed a/-Haya’, the reality of which is that it is a mannerism that prevents one from committing vile actions and from being lax in fulfilling the rights of الله.’

* Inaba: returning, ibn al-Qayyim, Madani as-Salikin [1/467] said, ‘indbah comprises four matters: the love of الله, submission to Him, turning to Him, and turning away from everything besides Him. A person cannot be said to “penitent” unless he meets all four requirements and the explanation of the Salaf to this word revolves around this. The word also carries the meaning of quickness, returning and precedence; therefore the penitent is rushing to do that which would please his Lord, turning back to Him at every moment and foremost in doing that which He loves.’

Fourth Element: Station of Excellence (al-ihsan) and Vigilance of الله

of devils, casts away the doubts and commits the heart and the mind to become devoted to الله alone.

How close a servant is from his Lord depends on his share from the state of thsan, and because of which, the reward and rank of salah differ from one to another so that the difference between the salah of one person and another can be like the distance between heavens and earth though both perform the sa/ah similarly.

Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of الله

It is to acknowledge the infinite blessing for being at the service of الله. That your heart and body are deployed to serve Him [alone] - and are only for الله, whose Grace and Favour were conferred upc’ vou, to facilitate for you being in such a state [of servitude]. Had it not been for الله’s Grace and Favour upon you, nothing of that would have occurred, which is something that the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ (#8) acknowledged when they used to repeat a line of poetry, that reads as follows: .

‘By الله, was it not for الله, neither would we have been guided, nor able to give charity nor perform the prayer’

الله, Most High, said:

i Kesuaeitess of (Zithacieg 2s

“They consider it a favour to you that they have ac-

Fifth Element: Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of الله

cepted Islam. Say, ‘Do not consider your Islam a favour to me. Rather, الله has conferred favour upon you

that He has guided you to the faith, if you should be truthful.”

It is الله who permitted a servant, to become a believer in the first instance. It is Him who facilitated for people to pray, just as Prophet Ibrahim ——s said in the ayah,

OF bas cA oy Ee Sere roe eel onanoy

“Our Lord!, And make us Muslims [in submission] to

You and from our descendants a Muslim nation fin submission] to You”

“My Lord, make me one who establislicrs the prayer, and [many] from my descendants.”

It is therefore a fact that it’s the favour (a/-minna) of الله that He conferred upon His slaves to worship and obey Him. Indeed,

this is from the greatest of الله’s graces upon His servant: الله, Most High, said,

“And whatever you have of favour - it is from الله”

“And [remember] when your Lord proclaimed, ‘If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favour]; but if

you deny, indeed, My punishment is severe.” [Fussilat (41): 7]

This element by far is one of the greatest elements and most beneficial for the servant of الله; so the more profound the Tawhid of a person is, the more perfected his share from this element will be. One of the benefits attained from this element is that it prevents the heaft from feeling egoistic about the good deeds performed because when a person acknowledges and affirms that الله, Most High, is the One who guided the servant to Him and that it is His favour upon the servant that he is a believer, he will consequently no: ' = fascinated by his good deeds or even feel superior to other peopie because of that; therefore all such debased feelings are instantly removed from his heart and so neither does he talk about them nor feel better than others because of them; and this is the nature of any accepted good deed.

From the other benefits of acknowledging الله’s favour upon us, is that it makes a person praise the One really entitled and deserving of praise; not praise one self but rather acknowledges the fact that all the praise is due to الله alone just as one acknowledges the fact that the grace he basks in and the bounties one is blessed with are from الله alone. This benefit manifests the perfection (tamam) of one’s tawhid of الله.

That being said, a person will never have a firm foothold in the rank (maqam) of tawhid, until after one acknowledges and experi-

Fifth Element: Acknowledgment of the Favour (al-minna) of الله

ences all what has been said. As soon as one comes to cognize this, one deeply roots his footing on its ground, and as soon as the heart experiences that, one will then reap its fruit that no worldly grace or blessing can ever match, i.e. a person will then lavish in the (a/-mababba) love and intimacy (a/-uns) of الله while basking in the amiability of His company and longing (a/-shawq) to the moment of meeting Him, and luxuriate in His remembrance and worship. Truly, there is no good in life for a person when the path leading to all that is blocked and when the heart is shunned from all that as a person of this kind is as الله described in the ayah,

“Let them eat and enjoy sche and be diverted by [false] hope, for they are going to know.”

Sixth Element: Blaming One’s Self for Inadequate Efforts

It is to maintain the feeling that your endeavours and efforts to worship الله are always inadequate; and that the right of الله upon you is greater; therefore your obedience, worship and servitude (a/-‘wbiidiya) must be greater than what you perform, as the level of you: _vitude to الله must befit His Glory and Power.

If the servants and subjects of kings exhibit high regards (a/- ta’zim), respect (al-ibtiram), reverence (a/-tangir), awe (al-khashiya), dread (a/-mahaba) and sincerity (a/-nush) towards their masters to the extent they dedicate their body-limbs and Learts to serve them, then it befits more that the King of all kings (Malik al-Maluk) and the Lord of the heavens and the earth be treated not only in a similar manner but far more superior.

If a servant (a/-‘abd) acknowledges that his state of servitude (a/- “budiya) does not fulfil, or is even close to fulfilling the due rights of الله upon him. A person‘will then realise their shortcomings and will thus hasten to seek His forgiveness, apologise for ones deficiencies, inadequate worship and obedience. It is for this reason we are in more need of His forgiveness due to our inadequate worship and obedience than we are in need of asking His reward

Sixth Element: Blaming One’s Self for Inadequate E; sfforts

for the worship and obedience we have performed. There is no pride or credit to be claimed by a person, even if one was able to fulfil all the requirements of his state of servitude (al-‘ubidiya) to الله, Most High, because in that case one would only be fulfill ling the natural (role) of his status as a servant to his Master, الله Glorified be He, requires. It is obvious that if a servant served his master then he requested from his master a reward for his service, people would have deemed him a.fool though in the true terms and sense of reality he is nota truly a slave of another man since mankind is owned and is wholly a servant of الله alone from all aspects.
